title: Pedicularis dolichosiphon
taxonomy: Pedicularis dolichosiphon is a species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. It belongs to the genus Pedicularis and is native to the mountainous regions of China. The plant's classification places it within the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, and class Magnoliopsida. Pedicularis dolichosiphon is a parasitic plant, obtaining its nutrients from the surrounding vegetation. Its classification is further detailed in the APG III system as an Orobanchaceae species within the order Lamiales.

intro: Pedicularis dolichosiphon, also known as Long-spurred Pedicularis, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Orobanchaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever reduction, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities, making it a valuable resource for alleviating symptoms associated with colds, flu, and other respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Pedicularis dolichosiphon include iridoid glycosides, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Pedicularis dolichosiphon is often used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and capsules, which are used to treat conditions like cough, fever, and bronchitis.
